summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened his sermon by reading from John 4, where Jesus offers the Samaritan woman 'living water.' He titled the message 'Why You So Thirsty,' addressing the deep spiritual and relational dissatisfaction he observes in culture and the church. Pastor Travis contrasted being 'single for your savior' with being 'single and psycho' or 'single for Satan,' and similarly challenged those in relationships to pursue righteousness. He emphasized that neither singleness nor marriage is the ultimate source of fulfillment; that satisfaction comes only from a relationship with Jesus Christ, the living water. Drawing from Genesis, he explained that marriage is a sacred, God-created gift where two become 'one flesh,' a union that requires mutual submission and selfless service, not a 50/50 arrangement. He warned that selfishness is the root of broken relationships and challenged men, in particular, to lead their families Godly lives as examples. Referencing Ecclesiastes, he noted the emptiness of chasing worldly pleasures. The call was for everyone, single or married, to 'seek first the kingdom of God' (Matthew 6:33), build their lives on Christ, and find their true thirst quenched in Him alone. He concluded with an invitation to surrender to Jesus and experience the contentment only He provides.
mainPoints: Ultimate satisfaction and the quenching of spiritual thirst are found only in a relationship with Jesus Christ, the living water, not in marital status or worldly pursuits.
Marriage is a sacred gift from God defined by mutual submission and selfless service (out-serving one another), where two become one flesh, and it must be stewarded well.
Singleness is also a gift from God, providing unique opportunity for undivided devotion to the Lord's work, and should be a time of chasing God, not desperate pursuit of a partner.

versesMentioned.text: When a Samaritan woman came to draw water, Jesus said to her, 'Will you give me a drink?'
The Samaritan woman said to him, 'You are a Jew and I am a Samaritan woman. How can you ask me for a drink?' (For Jews do not associate with Samaritans.)
Jesus answered her, 'If you knew the gift of God and who it is that asks you for a drink, you would have asked him and he would have given you living water.'

Jesus answered, 'Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again, but whoever drinks the water I give them will never thirst. Indeed, the water I give them will become in them a spring of water welling up to eternal life.'
The Lord God said, 'It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make a helper suitable for him.'

So the Lord God caused the man to fall into a deep sleep; and while he was sleeping, he took one of the man’s ribs and then closed up the place with flesh. Then the Lord God made a woman from the rib he had taken out of the man, and he brought her to the man. The man said, 'This is now bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh; she shall be called ‘woman,’ for she was taken out of man.' That is why a man leaves his father and mother and is united to his wife, and they become one flesh.
He who finds a wife finds what is good and receives favor from the Lord.
But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.

Submit to one another out of reverence for Christ. Wives, submit yourselves to your own husbands as you do to the Lord. For the husband is the head of the wife as Christ is the head of the church, his body, of which he is the Savior. Now as the church submits to Christ, so also wives should submit to their husbands in everything. Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ loved the church and gave himself up for her to make her holy, cleansing her by the washing with water through the word, and to present her to himself as a radiant church, without stain or wrinkle or any other blemish, but holy and blameless. In this same way, husbands ought to love their wives as their own bodies. He who loves his wife loves himself. After all, no one ever hated their own body, but they feed and care for their body, just as Christ does the church— for we are members of his body. 'For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be united to his wife, and the two will become one flesh.' This is a profound mystery—but I am talking about Christ and the church. However, each one of you also must love his wife as he loves himself, and the wife must respect her husband.
Unless the Lord builds the house, the builders labor in vain. Unless the Lord watches over the city, the guards stand watch in vain.

I would like you to be free from concern. An unmarried man is concerned about the Lord’s affairs—how he can please the Lord. But a married man is concerned about the affairs of this world—how he can please his wife— and his interests are divided. An unmarried woman or virgin is concerned about the Lord’s affairs: Her aim is to be devoted to the Lord in both body and spirit. But a married woman is concerned about the affairs of this world—how she can please her husband. I am saying this for your own good, not to restrict you, but that you may live in a right way in undivided devotion to the Lord.
If you declare with your mouth, 'Jesus is Lord,' and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.

Monday.scripture: John 4:13-14 (NIV) - 'Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again, but whoever drinks the water I give them will never thirst.'
Monday.action: Spend 10 minutes in quiet reflection. Write down one area of life (e.g., relationships, career, status) where you've been seeking satisfaction apart from God.
Monday.prayer: Lord, show me where I am drinking from broken wells. Help me to turn to you, the living water, for my true satisfaction.

Tuesday.scripture: Matthew 6:33 (NIV) - 'But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.'
Tuesday.action: Before checking your phone or starting your day, read one chapter from the Gospels (Matthew, Mark, Luke, or John).
Tuesday.prayer: God, I choose to seek you first today. Align my desires with your kingdom and help me trust that you will provide what I need.

Wednesday.scripture: Psalm 127:1 (NIV) - 'Unless the Lord builds the house, the builders labor in vain.'
Wednesday.action: Evaluate one key relationship or project. Ask: 'Am I building this according to God's principles, or my own?' Make one adjustment to align it more with God's word.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, be the architect of my life, my family, and my work. I surrender my plans to you. Build what you desire.

Thursday.scripture: Ephesians 5:21 (NIV) - 'Submit to one another out of reverence for Christ.'
Thursday.action: Intentionally out-serve someone today. Do a chore for a family member, encourage a coworker, or perform a secret act of kindness.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, you served us unto death. Give me a heart to serve others selflessly today, doing it as an act of worship to you.

Friday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 7:32, 34 (NIV) - 'An unmarried man is concerned about the Lord’s affairs... An unmarried woman... is concerned about the Lord’s affairs.'
Friday.focus: The gift of your current season.
Friday.action: Whether single or married, identify one way you can use your current time and freedom to serve in your church or community. Take one step toward doing it.
Friday.prayer: God, thank you for the season I am in. Show me how to use my time and gifts for your glory with undivided devotion.

Saturday.scripture: Romans 10:9 (NIV) - 'If you declare with your mouth, 'Jesus is Lord,' and believe in your heart... you will be saved.'
Saturday.action: If you have never fully surrendered to Christ, pray the prayer Pastor Travis outlined. If you have, write a short prayer of renewed commitment to making Jesus the Lord of every area of your life.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, I believe you are Lord. I surrender my life to you. Forgive my sins, fill me with your Spirit, and be the source of my life and satisfaction forever.
